mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which translates to higher corporate earnings expectations and drives S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with RSAFS year-over-year growth over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a weakening labor market, which typically precedes an economic slowdown and negatively impacts corporate profits, leading to lower S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is low and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is also low, it indicates an accommodative monetary environment that supports equity valuations by lowering the cost of capital and encouraging investment, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If, during periods of low inflation and low FEDFUNDS, the S&P 500's 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive or show a negative correlation.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) during an economic expansion indicate robust consumer and business confidence, which translates into broader economic growth and positive short-term market sentiment, driving S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1000 (thousands of units) on a 3-month moving average
failure_condition: If, specifically during periods identified as economic expansion, a high HOUST reading does not lead to positive 5-day S&P 500 returns more than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects healthy manufacturing and supply-side activity, signaling strong corporate earnings potential and supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward returns do not exhibit a consistent positive correlation with the year-over-year growth rate of INDPRO across different economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A significant deceleration in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) growth indicates a cooling labor market, which can signal an impending economic slowdown and lead to reduced corporate profitability, negatively impacting S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth rate falls below 100,000 persons per month
failure_condition: If, after the trigger, the S&P 500's 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth (GDPC1) is strong and retail sales (RSAFS) are also robust, it signifies broad-based economic strength and consumer confidence, which provides a strong fundamental backdrop for equity markets, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annual growth > 2.0% and RSAFS annual growth > 3.0%
failure_condition: If, under conditions of strong GDP and retail sales growth, the S&P 500's 60-day forward returns are negative or flat on average over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high inflation, an increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals a hawkish monetary policy aimed at curbing price pressures, which can negatively impact corporate earnings and investor sentiment, leading to short-term negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by > 0.25% in a month
failure_condition: If, during periods where CPIAUCSL annual growth is above 3% and FEDFUNDS increases, the S&P 500's 5-day forward returns are predominantly positive.
